title: Bridge Paper: Cerebellar circuitry and auditory verbal hallucinations: An integrative synthesis and perspective.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** auditory verbal hallucination, hallucination proneness, voice hearing, inner speech, corollary discharge, source monitoring

Paper: Cerebellar circuitry and auditory verbal hallucinations: An integrative synthesis and perspective.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2020.08.004
Abstract: Auditory verbal hallucinations (AVH) - experienced as voice hearing independent of a corresponding external sound source - are a cardinal symptom of psychosis. Approximately 6-13% of healthy individuals also experience voice hearing. Despite numerous attempts to explain the neurofunctional mechanisms underlying AVH, they remain notoriously unexplained. However, evidence relates AVH to mechanistic changes in the forward model. This review synthesizes behavioral and neuroimaging studies exploring
